title: SPARRING — public research artifacts (Resolver governance report and framework)
description: Curated public reproducibility artifacts for research on SPARRING, a methodology for structured adversarial AI deliberation. This release accompanies the empirical report on SPARRING's Resolver (papers/2026-resolver-governance/): the locked pre-registration and amendment ledger, the output-preference rater study's analysis code and frozen aggregate results, the mechanism-audit and seeded-defect governance pilots, and the grounding verifier with its self-tests, plus the shared framework specification and guides. Exposure posture: study raters are de-identified (opaque labels and user ids only, no names/emails); the mechanism-audit corpus is the author's proprietary codebases, included only as derived findings/codings (never source); bulky unaudited per-run transcripts are omitted (codings, results, and code reproduce every reported number).
